executiveSummary.text: Greatland Resources is a UK-listed gold explorer and mining company focused on the prolific Pilbara region of Western Australia and, critically, a flagship partnership with Newmont at the Blackhill joint venture. The stock is up 11% today (8 April 2026) following news of a Newmont mining lease ratification - a material de-risking event that validates the economic viability of the Blackhill project. At 740p with a P/E of 8x, the market is pricing this as a plain-jane micro-cap gold miner. The nea

businessModel.text: Greatland Resources acquires, explores, and develops gold and base metal properties in Australia, primarily in the Pilbara craton - one of the world's most geologically prospective greenstone belt regions. The company's strategy is to make early-stage discoveries and bring them to a point where a major miner (like Newmont) will earn into or acquire the asset. This is a classic junior miner model: farm-in agreements transfer development capital burden to the major while Greatland retains exposure to upside. Revenue is entirely pre-production. The company is cash flow negative and will remain so until Blackhill or another asset reaches commercial production. The value driver is the valuation of in-ground resources relative to the cost of discovery and development - measured in ounces per share, not earnings multiples. The Newmont partnership is the central pillar. Newmont's ratification of the Blackhill mining lease is a signal that the geological work has cleared a major hurdle. Newmont's technical and financial backing substantially reduces the binary risk that typically kills micro-cap explorers.

financialSnapshot.text: Live price: 740p (GBX) - up 11.03% today 52-week range: 202p - 799.34p (currently ~93rd percentile) Market cap: ~£978M (based on ~131M shares outstanding) P/E: 8.04× - anomalously low for an explorer; reflects partial value recognition EPS: implied ~92p based on P/E Currency: GBX (pence sterling) Exchange: LSE Premium Listing The 8x P/E is misleading for a pre-revenue explorer. The market appears to be assigning some discovery value implicitly through the Newmont partnership re-rating. Be cautious treating this as a "cheap" valuation - explorers don't earn P/Es until they produce.

recentCatalysts.text: Newmont Blackhill mining lease ratification (April 2026): The material catalyst. Newmont formally ratifying the mining lease is a de-risking event that validates the economic and regulatory viability of Blackhill. This is not a drill result - it is a permitting and partnership milestone. Blackhill Bankable Feasibility Study (BFS): The next major catalyst. A completed BFS with robust economics would significantly de-risk the project and could trigger Newmont to increase its stake or move toward production. Timeline should be monitored for RNS updates. Gold price environment: Gold has been in a structural bull market driven by central bank demand, geopolitical risk, and fiscal deficit concerns. A sustained gold price above $2,500/oz materially improves the NPV of any Australian gold development. Drilling results from other Greatland targets: The company has multiple other prospects in the Pilbara pipeline. Any new high-grade intercept would be a re-rating catalyst independent of Blackhill.
items: Newmont Blackhill mining lease ratification (April 2026): The material catalyst. Newmont formally ratifying the mining lease is a de-risking event that validates the economic and regulatory viability of Blackhill. This is not a drill result - it is a permitting and partnership milestone.

Blackhill Bankable Feasibility Study (BFS): The next major catalyst. A completed BFS with robust economics would significantly de-risk the project and could trigger Newmont to increase its stake or move toward production. Timeline should be monitored for RNS updates.

Gold price environment: Gold has been in a structural bull market driven by central bank demand, geopolitical risk, and fiscal deficit concerns. A sustained gold price above $2,500/oz materially improves the NPV of any Australian gold development.
Drilling results from other Greatland targets: The company has multiple other prospects in the Pilbara pipeline. Any new high-grade intercept would be a re-rating catalyst independent of Blackhill.

thesisEvaluation.text: Scenario Probability Price Target Key Drivers Bull 25% 900p+ BFS delivers robust economics; Newmont increases stake; production decision made; gold at $2,800+; major exploration success at other targets Base 55% 700-850p Blackhill progresses steadily; BFS on track; Newmont remains committed but no escalation; gold stays $2,400-2,700; stock consolidates Bear 20% 450-650p BFS disappointing economics; Newmont steps back; gold falls; equity raise required; dilution; exploration drilling disappoints Weighted price target: 25% × 900 + 55% × 775 + 20% × 570 = 765p . Slight upside from 740p. Conviction: 57/100 . HOLD - SPECULATIVE.

keyRisks.text: 1 Execution risk at Blackhill: A ratified mining lease is not a producing mine. The BFS must demonstrate economics that justify Newmont's continued investment. Any slippage in capex estimates or recovery rates could stall the project. 2 Gold price sensitivity: The entire junior gold sector is leveraged to the gold price. If gold corrects sharply (e.g., on a rate shock or US dollar strength), Greatland's NAV falls regardless of geological quality. 3 Newmont strategic decisions: Newmont is the operator and far-and-away the most important counterparty. Any change in Newmont's Australian portfolio strategy, capital allocation priorities, or appetite for M&A could directly affect Greatland's trajectory. 4 Equity raise risk: Pre-production miners regularly require capital raises. At 740p near the 52-week high, the company is in a better position to raise than at 400p - but dilution remains a risk if the BFS reveals higher-than-expected capex. 5 FX and currency risk: The company reports in GBP but operates in AUD. Gold is priced in USD. The GBP/AUD cross rate introduces an additional layer of volatility unrelated to the underlying asset quality.

risks.risk: 1 Execution risk at Blackhill: A ratified mining lease is not a producing mine. The BFS must demonstrate economics that justify Newmont's continued investment. Any slippage in capex estimates or recovery rates could stall the project.

2 Gold price sensitivity: The entire junior gold sector is leveraged to the gold price. If gold corrects sharply (e.g., on a rate shock or US dollar strength), Greatland's NAV falls regardless of geological quality.

3 Newmont strategic decisions: Newmont is the operator and far-and-away the most important counterparty. Any change in Newmont's Australian portfolio strategy, capital allocation priorities, or appetite for M&A could directly affect Greatland's trajectory.

4 Equity raise risk: Pre-production miners regularly require capital raises. At 740p near the 52-week high, the company is in a better position to raise than at 400p - but dilution remains a risk if the BFS reveals higher-than-expected capex.

5 FX and currency risk: The company reports in GBP but operates in AUD. Gold is priced in USD. The GBP/AUD cross rate introduces an additional layer of volatility unrelated to the underlying asset quality.

whoShouldOwn.text: Should own: Speculative investors with a high risk tolerance who want direct gold sector exposure with a major-miner partnership milestone recently confirmed. Those who understand the junior miner lifecycle and can tolerate binary outcomes and illiquidity. Anyone who believes the gold bull market is still early. Should avoid: Conservative or income-focused investors. Anyone who cannot tolerate a 30-50% drawdown in a single bad news cycle. Investors who need earnings visibility or dividend income. Those who mistake the 8x P/E for "cheap" without understanding it reflects partial asset value, not a producing business.

recommendation.text: HOLD - SPECULATIVE at 740p. The Newmont mining lease ratification is a genuine inflection point. The stock has reacted strongly and is now within 8% of the 52-week high. Wait for the BFS before adding materially. Trim if gold retreats or if the market gets ahead of the fundamentals.

entryExit.text: Below 650p - accumulation zone for speculative accounts. 650p-750p - hold. Above 800p - trim. Key triggers: BFS publication,. Newmont farm-in milestone announcements, gold price moves above $2,700 or below $2,300. Conviction Trend Latest conviction: 57/100 .
